brisk.name: Brisk Teaching
brisk.studentNote: Staff use only — not approved for direct student use

tips.title: Turn Any YouTube Video Into an Instant Quiz with Brisk

video.title: Brisk Bites 3 — AI Quiz Generator
video.description: A bite-sized Brisk Teaching PD video walking through the AI Quiz Generator — point the Brisk extension at a webpage, PDF, or YouTube video and generate a standards-aligned quiz with an answer key already built in.

tips.whyItMatters: Skip writing a comprehension check from scratch. Point Brisk at the video or article you already assigned and get a ready-to-grade quiz — multiple choice, short answer, or open-ended, delivered to a Google Form or Doc — in under a minute. Handy for flipped-classroom check-ins, exit tickets, or catch-up work for an absent student.
tips.complianceNote: PII-secure and approved for staff use. Brisk is not currently approved for direct student use in GUHSD — assign the generated quiz through your own Google Form or Doc.

tips.title: Differentiate Any Reading in Three Prompts, Not Three Prep Periods

video.title: Using Gemini for Google Workspace as an Educator
video.description: A Google for Education walkthrough of how teachers use Gemini inside the Workspace tools they already have open — drafting materials, adjusting reading levels, and building differentiated versions of an assignment without leaving Docs or Slides.

tips.whyItMatters: Every class has a range of readers. Ask Gemini to take one passage and generate a below-level, on-level, and extension version — same content, three entry points — in the time it used to take to rewrite one paragraph by hand. Students get material they can actually access; you get your prep period back.
tips.complianceNote: PII-secure and approved for staff and student use with your guhsd.net account. Keep prompts free of student names or identifying details.

tips.title: Feedback That Lands: Skip the Generic 'Good Job' with Brisk

video.title: How to Use Brisk: Brisk Teaching Overview
video.description: A guided tour of the Brisk Chrome extension inside a student's Google Doc — choosing a feedback style (Glows & Grows, rubric-based, or next-steps) and generating specific, actionable comments in place of a rubric score alone.

tips.whyItMatters: Targeted feedback is one of the highest-leverage moves a teacher can make — and one of the most time-consuming to do well for 150 students. Brisk drafts specific, rubric-aligned comments in seconds so you can spend your energy on the students who need a real conversation, not retyping the same note for the fifth time.
tips.complianceNote: PII-secure and approved for staff use. Brisk is not currently approved for direct student use in GUHSD — the teacher remains the one giving feedback.

tips.title: Turn Any Unit Packet into a Study Partner with NotebookLM

video.title: An Introduction to Google NotebookLM
video.description: A short demonstration of uploading course materials into NotebookLM and using its source-grounded chat to generate summaries and answer questions — with every answer citing back to the exact page it came from.

tips.whyItMatters: Upload your unit's readings, slides, and notes, and NotebookLM only answers from what you gave it — no outside internet content, no invented facts. Students can ask it to quiz them or explain a confusing paragraph, and every answer links back to the source. It's a study partner that can't wander off-topic.
tips.complianceNote: PII-secure and approved for staff and student use with your guhsd.net account. Keep student-identifying records (IEPs, grades, behavior notes) out of any uploaded source.
